fields.title: Email Basics
fields.description: Teaches the fundamentals of identifying email phishing attempts, focusing on URLs, sender addresses, and false urgency.

fields.prompt_text: Is this email legitimate or a phishing attempt?
fields.subject: URGENT: Password Expiry in 24 hours
fields.body:
Dear User,

Your account password will expire in 24 hours. Please click the link below to verify your account and keep your current password.

Thank you,
IT Support

fields.feedback_text: Correct! This is a phishing attempt. The sender domain 'company-update-portal.com' is typosquatted or unaffiliated with official services. It uses psychological triggers like false urgency or fear, and lacks personalization (e.g., 'Dear User'). Always hover over links before clicking.

Incorrect. This is a phishing attempt. The sender domain 'company-update-portal.com' is typosquatted or unaffiliated with official services. It uses psychological triggers like false urgency or fear, and lacks personalization (e.g., 'Dear User'). Always hover over links before clicking.

fields.prompt_text: Is this email legitimate or a phishing attempt?
fields.subject: Action Required: Updated Company Policy
fields.body:
Hello,

Please review the updated employee handbook and code of conduct. You are required to sign the acknowledgment document by end of day.

Regards,
Human Resources

fields.feedback_text: Correct! This is a phishing attempt. The sender domain 'secure-login-auth.net' is typosquatted or unaffiliated with official services. It uses psychological triggers like false urgency or fear, and lacks personalization (e.g., 'Dear User'). Always hover over links before clicking.

fields.prompt_text: Is this email legitimate or a phishing attempt?
fields.subject: Scheduled Maintenance Notice
fields.body:
Hi team,

We will be performing routine server maintenance this Saturday from 2 AM to 4 AM. Internal portals may be briefly unavailable. No action is required on your part.

Thanks,
IT

fields.feedback_text: Incorrect. This is a legitimate internal email. The sender domain matches your company's actual domain, it addresses you appropriately, and there is no false urgency or suspicious request for credentials.
